name: TitleMax Title Loans
description_short: TitleMax Title Loans is located at 3236 E McDowell Rd, Phoenix, AZ 85008 in the Camelback East Village neighborhood. Google reviewers rate the branch 5.0 stars across 636 reviews. Call +1 602-281-7714.
description_long:
TitleMax is an emergency lending company operating title loan stores across the United States, with a established presence in Memphis, Tennessee since February 2007. The Summer Avenue location has served the greater Memphis area for over 15 years and operates during extended hours (9am-7pm most weekdays, 9am-4pm Saturday). The company positions itself as an accessible alternative to traditional credit-based lending by focusing on vehicle equity rather than credit scores.

The company offers two primary products: Title Pledges (secured loans using car titles) and Flex Lines of Credit (both secured and unsecured). Title pledges allow customers to borrow against their vehicle's value while keeping and driving their car. The Flex Line of Credit functions as a revolving credit product that customers can draw from all at once or incrementally up to their limit. They also offer unsecured personal lines of credit for customers in qualifying states (AZ, DE, ID, KS, MO, MS, SC, TN, TX, UT, WI). All products emphasize quick approval and same-day cash availability.

The Memphis TN 9 location differentiates through demonstrated customer service quality (481 Google reviews averaging 5.0 stars with specific staff praise), bilingual services, and competitive positioning on rates. Staff members like Nicole, Joyce, and Jonathan receive individual mention for professionalism and extra effort in customer education. The store's location one mile west of Highland Street across from AutoZone provides geographic accessibility for the Memphis market.

However, title loans carry inherent risks common to secured lending: failure to repay risks vehicle repossession, and the APR and fee structures are not disclosed on this webpage, preventing transparent cost assessment. While the company accepts most credit types, the qualification requirements (valid ID, clear vehicle title, proof of income/bank statements) and vehicle value requirements create barriers for some consumers. The product category itself—high-cost emergency lending—suggests this serves consumers in financial distress rather than providing optimal financial solutions.
pros: Same-day cash availability with quick approval process
Accepts most credit types; credit score not primary qualifier
Keep and continue driving your vehicle while using title as collateral
Extended store hours (9am-7pm weekdays) for working customer access
Bilingual services available at this location
Established 15+ year history at this specific Memphis location
Perfect 5.0-star Google rating (481 reviews) with documented staff professionalism
cons: Vehicle repossession risk if loan is not repaid—title serves as security
APR, fees, and complete pricing terms not disclosed on website
Requires clear vehicle title and valid government ID—excludes those without titled vehicles
Personal lines of credit limited to 10 specific states, excluding most of the U.S.
As title loan provider, inherently positions as high-cost emergency solution rather than optimal financial product
best_for: Vehicle owners facing unexpected emergencies needing cash within 24 hours
Consumers with damaged credit who cannot qualify for traditional bank loans
Memphis-area residents with flexible daytime/evening availability for in-person application
Borrowers who can reliably repay within short-term loan period and afford secured lending rates
diagnosis: TitleMax Memphis TN 9 is designed for vehicle owners with immediate cash needs who cannot access traditional credit quickly. The critical caveat is that title loans are high-cost emergency products with vehicle repossession risk; customers should exhaust other options first and ensure they can reliably repay before securing the loan against their car.
services: Title Pledges (secured loans using vehicle title as collateral)
Flex Line of Credit (secured, revolving credit)
Unsecured Personal Lines of Credit (online and in-store)
Loan management portal (separate for title loans vs. personal loans)
Credit checks and vehicle valuation assessment
Flexible payment options (lump sum or incremental drawdowns)
